english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego, fratres, non potui vobis loqui quasi spiritualibus, sed quasi carnalibus. Tamquam parvulis in Christo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγώ, ἀδελφοί οὐκ ἠδυνήθην λαλῆσαι ὑμῖν ὡς πνευματικοῖς ἀλλ᾽ ὡς σαρκικοῖς ὡς νηπίοις ἐν χριστῷ
verses.king_james: And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, *even* as unto babes in Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: lac vobis potum dedi, non escam: nondum enim poteratis: sed nec nunc quidem potestis: adhuc enim carnales estis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γάλα ὑμᾶς ἐπότισα καὶ οὐ βρῶμα οὔπω γὰρ ἠδύνασθε ἀλλ᾽ οὖτε ἔτι νῦν δύνασθε
verses.king_james: I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able *to bear it*, neither yet now are ye able.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum enim sit inter vos zelus, et contentio: nonne carnales estis, et secundum hominem ambulatis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔτι γὰρ σαρκικοί ἐστε ὅπου γὰρ ἐν ὑμῖν ζῆλος καὶ ἔρις καὶ διχοστασίαι, οὐχὶ σαρκικοί ἐστε καὶ κατὰ ἄνθρωπον περιπατεῖτε
verses.king_james: For ye are yet carnal: for whereas *there is* among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum enim quis dicat: Ego quidem sum Pauli; alius autem: Ego Apollo: nonne homines estis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν γὰρ λέγῃ τις ἐγὼ μέν εἰμι παύλου ἕτερος δέ ἐγὼ ἀπολλῶ οὐχὶ σαρκικοί ἐστε
verses.king_james: For while one saith, I am of Paul; and another, I *am* of Apollos; are ye not carnal?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid igitur est Apollo? quid vero Paulus? ministri ejus, cui credidistis, ut unicuique sicut Dominus dedit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς οὖν ἐστιν παῦλος τίς δέ ἀπολλῶς ἀλλ᾽ ἢ διάκονοι δι᾽ ὧν ἐπιστεύσατε καὶ ἑκάστῳ ὡς ὁ κύριος ἔδωκεν
verses.king_james: Who then is Paul, and who *is* Apollos, but ministers by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego plantavi, Apollo rigavit: sed Deus incrementum dedit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ ἐφύτευσα ἀπολλῶς ἐπότισεν ἀλλ᾽ ὁ θεὸς ηὔξανεν
verses.king_james: I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque neque qui plantat est aliquid, neque qui rigat: sed qui incrementum dat, Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε οὔτε ὁ φυτεύων ἐστίν τι οὔτε ὁ ποτίζων ἀλλ᾽ ὁ αὐξάνων θεός
verses.king_james: So then neither is he that planteth any thing, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem plantat, et qui rigat, unum sunt. Unusquisque autem propriam mercedem accipiet, secundum suum laborem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ φυτεύων δὲ καὶ ὁ ποτίζων ἕν εἰσιν ἕκαστος δὲ τὸν ἴδιον μισθὸν λήψεται κατὰ τὸν ἴδιον κόπον
verses.king_james: Now he that planteth and he that watereth are one: and every man shall receive his own reward according to his own labour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dei enim sumus adjutores: Dei agricultura estis, Dei ædificatio estis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεοῦ γάρ ἐσμεν συνεργοί θεοῦ γεώργιον θεοῦ οἰκοδομή ἐστε
verses.king_james: For we are labourers together with God: ye are God's husbandry, *ye are* God's building.

verses.latin_vulgate: Secundum gratiam Dei, quæ data est mihi, ut sapiens architectus fundamentum posui: alius autem superædificat. Unusquisque autem videat quomodo superædificet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι ὡς σοφὸς ἀρχιτέκτων θεμέλιον τέθεικα, ἄλλος δὲ ἐποικοδομεῖ ἕκαστος δὲ βλεπέτω πῶς ἐποικοδομεῖ
verses.king_james: According to the grace of God which is given unto me, as a wise masterbuilder, I have laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fundamentum enim aliud nemo potest ponere præter id quod positum est, quod est Christus Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεμέλιον γὰρ ἄλλον οὐδεὶς δύναται θεῖναι παρὰ τὸν κείμενον ὅς ἐστιν ἰησοῦς ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: For other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem superædificat super fundamentum hoc, aurum, argentum, lapides pretiosos, ligna, fœnum, stipulam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις ἐποικοδομεῖ ἐπὶ τὸν θεμέλιον τοῦτον χρυσόν ἄργυρον λίθους τιμίους ξύλα χόρτον καλάμην
verses.king_james: Now if any man build upon this foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, stubble;

verses.latin_vulgate: uniuscujusque opus manifestum erit: dies enim Domini declarabit, quia in igne revelabitur: et uniuscujusque opus quale sit, ignis probabit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑκάστου τὸ ἔργον φανερὸν γενήσεται ἡ γὰρ ἡμέρα δηλώσει ὅτι ἐν πυρὶ ἀποκαλύπτεται καὶ ἑκάστου τὸ ἔργον ὁποῖόν ἐστιν τὸ πῦρ δοκιμάσει
verses.king_james: Every man's work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man's work of what sort it is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si cujus opus manserit quod superædificavit, mercedem accipiet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τινος τὸ ἔργον μενεῖ ὃ ἐπωκοδόμησεν μισθὸν λήψεται
verses.king_james: If any man's work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si cujus opus arserit, detrimentum patietur: ipse autem salvus erit, sic tamen quasi per ignem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τινος τὸ ἔργον κατακαήσεται ζημιωθήσεται αὐτὸς δὲ σωθήσεται οὕτως δὲ ὡς διὰ πυρός
verses.king_james: If any man's work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself shall be saved; yet so as by fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nescitis quia templum Dei estis, et Spiritus Dei habitat in vobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ οἴδατε ὅτι ναὸς θεοῦ ἐστε καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ θεοῦ οἰκεῖ ἐν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and *that* the Spirit of God dwelleth in you?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem templum Dei violaverit, disperdet illum Deus. Templum enim Dei sanctum est, quod estis vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις τὸν ναὸν τοῦ θεοῦ φθείρει φθερεῖ τοῦτον ὁ θεός ὁ γὰρ ναὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ἅγιός ἐστιν οἵτινές ἐστε ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which *temple* ye are.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo se seducat: si quis videtur inter vos sapiens esse in hoc sæculo, stultus fiat ut sit sapiens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδεὶς ἑαυτὸν ἐξαπατάτω εἴ τις δοκεῖ σοφὸς εἶναι ἐν ὑμῖν ἐν τῷ αἰῶνι τούτῳ μωρὸς γενέσθω ἵνα γένηται σοφός
verses.king_james: Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia enim hujus mundi, stultitia est apud Deum. Scriptum est enim: Comprehendam sapientes in astutia eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ σοφία τοῦ κόσμου τούτου μωρία παρὰ τῷ θεῷ ἐστιν γέγραπται γάρ ὁ δρασσόμενος τοὺς σοφοὺς ἐν τῇ πανουργίᾳ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own craftiness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum: Dominus novit cogitationes sapientium quoniam vanæ sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν κύριος γινώσκει τοὺς διαλογισμοὺς τῶν σοφῶν ὅτι εἰσὶν μάταιοι
verses.king_james: And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo itaque glorietur in hominibus. Omnia enim vestra sunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε μηδεὶς καυχάσθω ἐν ἀνθρώποις πάντα γὰρ ὑμῶν ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Therefore let no man glory in men. For all things are yours;

verses.latin_vulgate: sive Paulus, sive Apollo, sive Cephas, sive mundus, sive vita, sive mors, sive præsentia, sive futura: omnia enim vestra sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε παῦλος εἴτε ἀπολλῶς εἴτε κηφᾶς εἴτε κόσμος εἴτε ζωὴ εἴτε θάνατος εἴτε ἐνεστῶτα εἴτε μέλλοντα πάντα ὑμῶν ἐστιν,
verses.king_james: Whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come; all are yours;

verses.latin_vulgate: vos autem Christi: Christus autem Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ χριστοῦ χριστὸς δὲ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And ye are Christ's; and Christ *is* God's.
